$grounding: Grounded in: docs/design/ui-dual-tone-tokens.json (ADMIN tone tokens — this screen lives in apps/admin), apps/admin/app/lib/ui.tsx (AppShell/ThemeStyle bound to tone='admin'; ContentIcon for the empty state), apps/admin/app/lib/branding.ts (per-tenant accent via --lms-accent; admin fallback #6a8cff — never hard-coded), apps/admin/app/courses/new/page.tsx (the canonical admin create-form: AppShell + ghost back Button + PageHeader + isAdmin gate + warning Alert + Card.asg-form-card with .asg-section / .asg-grid-2 / .asg-actionbar scoped CSS + Field/Input/Textarea + server action submit), apps/admin/app/pages/page-editor.tsx (client-component + scoped-CSS + WAI-ARIA toolbar/contenteditable conventions, sanitize-html, aria-live status), apps/admin/app/api/uploads/route.ts + lib/pages-api signUpload (the SIGNED-UPLOAD BFF: POST /api/uploads {filename,contentType,sizeBytes} -> 201 {upload:{uploadUrl,blobUrl}}; client PUTs bytes to uploadUrl then uses blobUrl), and handshake feat-scorm-import-play.md §4.F route POST /scorm/packages + §4.H security guards + §6 follow-ups. ALL tokens REUSED from the admin tone; NO new palette. The only net-new behaviour (client-side .zip read to extract imsmanifest.xml + paste fallback) is flagged in openQuestions; everything else reuses existing @lms/ui components and the established admin form chrome.
goal: An org admin uploads a SCORM .zip and gets a launchable package created: they upload the archive via the existing signed-URL flow, provide the imsmanifest.xml (auto-extracted from the .zip client-side, or pasted/uploaded as a fallback), submit to create the package, and see the parsed result (title, SCORM version, launch href, mastery score) or a clear, recoverable error.
route: /scorm/import  (admin app; sits alongside /courses, /pages — links from a course's content section when topicId is provided as a query param)
informationHierarchy: Primary outcome: a launchable SCORM package was created (success summary card is the loudest thing once import succeeds)
The two inputs that produce it, in order: (1) the .zip archive, (2) the imsmanifest.xml (auto-extracted from the .zip, shown read-only; editable/pasteable as a fallback)
Submit affordance: 'Import package' + its busy/disabled state
Errors when parsing fails: which of invalid_manifest / no_launchable_resource / unsafe_href, in plain language, with what to do
Optional context: the destination topic/course (topicId) shown as read-only context when arriving from a course
Honest scope note: byte-level unzip + serving the package's runtime assets server-side is a documented follow-up — this screen imports the manifest + the archive's blobUrl
wiresTo: Step A (archive): POST /api/uploads { filename, contentType:'application/zip', sizeBytes } -> 201 { upload: { uploadUrl, blobUrl } }; client PUTs the .zip bytes to uploadUrl (existing signed-upload flow, application/zip already allow-listed). Step B (manifest): client reads imsmanifest.xml out of the selected .zip in-browser (or admin pastes/uploads it). Step C (import): a new admin BFF route POST /api/scorm/packages (re-checks isAdmin, forwards x-tenant-id) -> content service POST /scorm/packages { manifestXml, topicId?, blobUrl } -> 201 { package: { id, version, title, launchHref, masteryScore, blobUrl } } | 400 { error: 'invalid_manifest' | 'no_launchable_resource' | 'unsafe_href', message }.

designTokens.$note: ADMIN tone from docs/design/ui-dual-tone-tokens.json, exposed at runtime as --lms-* vars via ThemeStyle tone='admin'. accent is the TENANT accent via --lms-accent (admin fallback #6a8cff / token fallback #0E5FAE). Reference vars, never literals, in the build.

color.surface2: var(--lms-surface-2) /* #E9EDF3 — read-only manifest preview / code area fill */

typography.fontFamily: var(--lms-font-family) /* tenant font, else system-ui fallback */
typography.mono: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Consolas, monospace — ONLY for the read-only manifest XML preview and the launch href value; everything else uses the tenant font

space.$map: --lms-space-1..8 (4,8,12,16,24,32,48,64); reuse the courses/new .asg-* rhythm (form gap space-5, field gap space-4)

layout.containerMaxWidth: 1200px (AppShell .lms-shell__main); the import form itself caps at ~760px for comfortable single-column reading, centered in main
regions: main (ghost back Button -> PageHeader -> form Card / success Card)

composition.desktop: Single centered column ~760px. One Card.asg-form-card holds two stacked .asg-section blocks: 'Package archive' (dropzone) then 'Manifest' (read-only preview + paste fallback). .asg-actionbar pins Cancel + Import package bottom-right. On success the form Card is replaced by a ParsedPackageSummary Card (definition list of parsed fields) + a 'Done' / 'Import another' action row.
composition.tablet: Same single column, full container width minus margins.
composition.phone: Single column, full-width; dropzone, preview, and action buttons all stretch full-width (reuse .asg-actionbar stretch rule). No element exceeds viewport width at 360px; the XML preview scrolls within its own bordered region, never the page.

breakpoints.approach: mobile-first, fluid between breakpoints. The manifest XML preview uses overflow:auto + white-space:pre inside a bordered, role=region tabIndex=0 scroll container so long lines never push the page wide; all other text uses overflow-wrap:anywhere. Nothing overflows horizontally at 360px.

components.name: BackLink (reuse Button variant=ghost size=sm, like courses/new .asg-back)
components.purpose: Return to the originating context (course content section or SCORM list).
anatomy: ghost Button with leading arrow glyph
components.content: '<- Back to course content' when arriving with a topicId; otherwise '<- Back to courses'.
components.interactions: navigates back; real <a> via Button href

a11y.label: text label is the name; arrow glyph is decorative text

components.name: PageHeader (reuse @lms/ui)
components.purpose: Orient the admin and state scope honestly.
anatomy: h1 title
subtitle
components.content: title 'Import SCORM package' · subtitle 'Upload a SCORM 1.2 or 2004 archive and we'll read its manifest to create a launchable package. Serving the package's runtime files is coming soon — for now this records the manifest and the uploaded archive.'

components.name: ArchiveDropzone (NET-NEW small client control — uses native <input type=file accept='.zip,application/zip'>)
components.purpose: Select the SCORM .zip, immediately read imsmanifest.xml out of it in-browser, and (on submit) request a signed URL + PUT the bytes.
anatomy: Field label 'SCORM package (.zip)' required

A labelled drop target: bordered (1px var(--lms-border-strong), radius var(--lms-radius-sm)) region, min-height ~120px, dashed border in idle, that wraps a real <input type=file> (visually styled, not hidden from AT — label is the click target)
Idle copy: 'Drop your SCORM .zip here or choose a file. We'll read imsmanifest.xml automatically.'

Selected state: filename + human size + a 'Replace file' button; if extraction succeeded a small success note 'Found imsmanifest.xml', if not an inline warning 'Couldn't find imsmanifest.xml in this archive — paste it below.'
components.content: Help text: 'SCORM 1.2 or 2004. Max size per your plan. The archive is uploaded as-is; we don't unzip its assets server-side yet.'
states: focus-visible (3px var(--lms-focus) ring on the input/label)
components.interactions: Click/Enter/Space on the label opens the OS picker; drag-and-drop also accepts a file but the visible <button>/<input> is the accessible path (drag is an enhancement, never the only way). On selection the file is read client-side (zip entry imsmanifest.xml) to populate the manifest preview. Bytes are NOT uploaded until submit, to avoid orphan uploads on abandoned imports.

a11y.role: the native file input is the control; the dropzone is its styled label
a11y.label: explicit <label for> = 'SCORM package (.zip)'; selection result announced via an aria-live=polite status near the field
a11y.keyboard: fully operable via the native input (Tab to it, Enter/Space opens picker); drag-drop is additive only

components.name: ManifestPreviewAndFallback (Field + read-only preview + Textarea fallback)
components.purpose: Show the imsmanifest.xml that will be parsed (read-only when auto-extracted) and allow paste/edit as a fallback when extraction fails or the admin only has the XML.
anatomy: Field label 'imsmanifest.xml' required

When auto-extracted: a read-only preview — div role=region aria-label='Manifest XML (read-only)' tabIndex=0, bg var(--lms-surface-2), 1px var(--lms-border-strong), radius var(--lms-radius-sm), monospace 14px, max-height ~280px, overflow:auto, white-space:pre — plus an 'Edit / paste a different manifest' disclosure that swaps in the Textarea
Fallback / edit mode: a Textarea (reuse @lms/ui Textarea, monospace, rows ~12) the admin can paste into
Help text under the field
components.content: Help (auto): 'Read from your archive. This is what we'll parse to find the launch page and mastery score.' · Help (paste): 'Paste the contents of imsmanifest.xml. Max 1 MB. We don't process DTDs or external entities for security.'
states: empty (no manifest yet — preview hidden, paste prompt shown)
invalid (aria-invalid + danger border when import returns invalid_manifest)

responsive.phone: full-width; preview max-height ~200px; horizontal scroll INSIDE the region only

components.interactions: Disclosure toggles read-only preview <-> editable Textarea (aria-expanded). On import error the relevant field gets aria-invalid + error text via aria-describedby (e.g. for invalid_manifest / unsafe_href).

a11y.role: read-only preview is a labelled focusable scroll region; edit mode is a labelled <textarea>
a11y.label: Field <label> 'imsmanifest.xml'; preview region aria-label; errors via aria-describedby + aria-invalid
a11y.keyboard: preview region is focusable so keyboard users can scroll it; Textarea standard entry; disclosure toggles with Enter/Space

components.name: DestinationContext (read-only, only when arriving with a topicId)
components.purpose: Show which course/topic the package will attach to, without inventing a topic picker the platform can't yet drive from here.
anatomy: small captioned read-only row: 'Adding to' + topic/course label (or the topicId if no label is resolvable)
components.content: 'Adding to: {Course title} - {Topic title}' (or 'Adding to topic {topicId}' if only the id is known).
variants: no-topic (component omitted — package created unattached, see openQuestions)

components.name: ImportActionBar (reuse Button @lms/ui + .asg-actionbar layout)
components.purpose: Submit the import; provide busy + error feedback.
anatomy: Right-aligned row (border-top): [Cancel ghost] [Import package primary]
Inline status region (aria-live=polite) left of the buttons: 'Uploading archive...' -> 'Creating package...' ; failures surface as a top-of-form Alert role=alert
components.content: Buttons 'Cancel', 'Import package'. Status: 'Uploading archive...', 'Reading manifest and creating package...'. Import disabled until BOTH a .zip is selected AND a non-empty manifest exists.
states: submitting (Import shows busy + aria-busy, both buttons disabled to prevent double-submit)
error (buttons re-enabled; Alert above the form names the failure)

responsive.phone: buttons stretch full-width (.asg-actionbar stretch rule); status above them

components.interactions: On submit: (1) POST /api/uploads to get a signed URL, PUT the .zip; (2) POST /api/scorm/packages with { manifestXml, topicId?, blobUrl }. On 201 swap to ParsedPackageSummary; on 400 map error -> the matching field/Alert.

a11y.role: button group + aria-live=polite status; errors via role=alert
a11y.label: status announced politely; never silent failure
a11y.keyboard: logical order after the manifest field; Enter/Space activate

components.name: ParsedPackageSummary (reuse Card + Stack + Badge/Chip; success state)
components.purpose: Confirm the package was created and show exactly what was parsed, so the admin can verify before learners launch it.
anatomy: success Alert tone=success role=status 'SCORM package created.'

Card with an h2 'Parsed package' and a definition list (<dl>): Title, SCORM version (Badge '1.2' / '2004'), Launch page (launchHref, monospace, overflow-wrap), Mastery score (e.g. '80% (0.8)' or 'Not specified'), Archive (blobUrl, truncated/monospace, with a 'copy' affordance)
action row: [View / launch as learner -> opens the learner play surface for this package] [Import another -> resets the form]
components.content: Example — Title 'Workplace Safety Fundamentals', version '1.2', Launch page 'shared/launchpage.html', Mastery score '80% (0.80)', Archive '.../scorm/ws-safety.zip'. Mastery absent: 'Not specified in the manifest.'

responsive.phone: dl stacks term-over-detail; values wrap/scroll, never overflow

components.interactions: 'View / launch as learner' links to the learner play route for package id; 'Import another' clears state back to initial.

a11y.role: region; success via role=status; <dl> semantics for term/value pairs
a11y.label: h2 heading names the region; Badge version carries TEXT not color

components.name: ImportErrorAlert (reuse Alert @lms/ui)
components.purpose: Map each backend 400 reason to plain, recoverable guidance — never raw error codes.
anatomy: Alert tone=danger role=alert at top of form (and field-level aria-invalid where applicable)
title-ish lead + what-to-do sentence

content.invalid_manifest: 'We couldn't read this manifest. Check that you uploaded a valid imsmanifest.xml (SCORM 1.2 or 2004) and that it isn't truncated. Files with DTDs or external entities are rejected for security.'
content.no_launchable_resource: 'This manifest has no launchable page. SCORM packages need at least one resource with a launch file (an SCO). Re-export the package from your authoring tool and try again.'
content.unsafe_href: 'The launch path in this manifest isn't allowed (it points outside the package, e.g. an absolute URL or a path with ".."). Re-export the package so its launch file is a relative path inside the archive.'
content.upload-failed: 'Uploading the archive failed. Check your connection and the file size, then try Import again. Nothing was created.'
content.service-offline: 'The content service is unavailable. Start it (CONTENT_STORE=memory pnpm dev in services/content) to import packages.'

components.interactions: error is dismissible by fixing the input and re-submitting; focus moves to the Alert (or the offending field) on appearance so SR users hear it

a11y.label: meaning in text + inline-SVG danger icon, never color alone; offending field gets aria-invalid + aria-describedby
a11y.keyboard: Alert receives focus on appearance; offending field reachable

components.name: PermissionDenied (reuse courses/new pattern: PageHeader + Alert tone=warning)
components.purpose: Gate the screen by role exactly like the other admin create screens.
anatomy: PageHeader 'Not authorized'
Alert tone=warning with the signed-in user
components.content: 'You are signed in as {session.userId}, but your account does not hold an administrator role, so importing SCORM packages is unavailable.'

a11y.label: text + inline-SVG warning icon, never color alone

flows: Happy path: /scorm/import -> drop a SCORM .zip -> manifest auto-extracted and previewed read-only ('Found imsmanifest.xml') -> Import package -> 'Uploading archive...' (signed PUT) -> 'Creating package...' (POST /scorm/packages) -> 201 -> ParsedPackageSummary (Title / version Badge / launch href / mastery score / archive) + 'View / launch as learner' + 'Import another'.
Fallback (no manifest in zip / XML only): selection shows 'Couldn't find imsmanifest.xml' -> admin pastes XML into the Textarea fallback -> Import proceeds the same way.
Empty/initial: no archive yet -> Import disabled; dropzone shows idle copy; manifest section prompts to select an archive or paste XML.
Loading: RSC gate while session resolves; once mounted the client form is interactive.
Uploading: aria-live status 'Uploading archive...'; buttons disabled, aria-busy.
Error invalid_manifest: danger Alert + manifest field aria-invalid with the invalid_manifest copy; focus to Alert.
Error no_launchable_resource: danger Alert with re-export guidance.
Error unsafe_href: danger Alert explaining relative-path requirement (ties to security guard §4.H.2).
Error upload-failed: danger Alert + retry; explicitly 'Nothing was created'.
Error service-offline: warning copy with the local dev command.
Permission denied (non-admin): PageHeader 'Not authorized' + warning Alert; nothing fetched/created.
Reduced motion: dropzone hover/drag transitions and any upload progress shimmer respect prefers-reduced-motion.

accessibility.contrastMin: 4.5:1 body text / 3:1 large text, UI components & focus ring (admin tone verified: text 16.9:1, muted 6.6:1, focus 3.9-4.6:1)
accessibility.focusOrder: logical DOM order — back link -> PageHeader -> archive input -> manifest preview/disclosure (or Textarea) -> Cancel -> Import. On error, focus moves to the Alert or the offending field. Visible :focus-visible 3px var(--lms-focus) ring offset 2px on every interactive element.
accessibility.fileInput: real native <input type=file> with an explicit <label>; drag-and-drop is an ADDITIVE enhancement, never the only path; selection outcome announced via aria-live=polite.
accessibility.manifestPreview: read-only XML is a focusable role=region with aria-label so keyboard users can scroll it; it is NOT an editable field masquerading as readonly — paste/edit is a separate, clearly toggled Textarea.
accessibility.status: upload/import progress in an aria-live=polite region; errors via role=alert; success via role=status; SCORM version conveyed by Badge TEXT ('1.2'/'2004'), never color alone.
accessibility.motion: respect prefers-reduced-motion — disable dropzone transforms and progress shimmer; keep instant color state changes and a static focus ring.
accessibility.semantics: AppShell header/main landmarks; single h1 (PageHeader) -> h2 section/summary headings; parsed result is a <dl>; native file input + Textarea labelled via Field; aria only where native won't do.
accessibility.touchTargets: 44x44px minimum for the file label, disclosure, Cancel, Import, and summary actions.
accessibility.noColorOnly: version, success, and every error carry a text label (+ inline-SVG icon for alerts) in addition to tone.

openQuestions: NET-NEW: reading imsmanifest.xml out of the selected .zip in-browser needs a small client-side unzip (e.g. fflate / zip.js, ~tiny). frontend-dev to confirm the lightest dependency, OR ship the paste-only fallback first (admin pastes XML) and add auto-extract as a fast-follow. The paste path requires zero new deps and fully satisfies the backend contract today. Recommend: ship paste-first + auto-extract behind the same Field if a dep is approved.

NEW BFF ROUTE: this screen needs an admin BFF route POST /api/scorm/packages (mirror app/api/uploads/route.ts: re-check isAdmin, forward x-tenant-id to the content service POST /scorm/packages). Confirm route placement + that the existing pages-api/dashboard lib is where the fetch helper should live. frontend-dev builds it; security-agent gates it.

TOPIC/DESTINATION: topicId is optional in the backend. From a course content section we can pass it via query param; there is no topic PICKER on this screen (the platform can't yet enumerate selectable SCORM topics from here). Confirm: arrive-with-topicId only, else create unattached? (Design assumes read-only DestinationContext when topicId present, omitted otherwise.)

ROLE GATE: matches courses/new isAdmin(session). Branch feat/admin-signin-role-gating is tightening admin role-gating — confirm SCORM import is org_admin+ (not instructor) for #31, so the permission-denied copy stays accurate.

FOLLOW-UP (honest scope, per handshake §6): server-side unzip + serving the package's runtime assets (byte-serving launchHref under blobUrl) is NOT in #31. The PageHeader subtitle and dropzone help state this plainly. Confirm the exact wording with product so we don't over-promise.

MAX SIZE: backend caps manifestXml at 1 MB and .zip size is bounded by upload limits. Surface the real configured .zip max in the dropzone help if it's available to the client; otherwise keep 'Max size per your plan.'
